title: A Tardy Apology - II
content:
You ask me, friend,
    Why I don't send
    The long since due-and-paid-for numbers;
    Why, songless, I
    As drunken lie
    Abandoned to Lethean slumbers.

    Long time ago
    (As well you know)
    I started in upon that carmen;
    My work was vain,--
    But why complain?
    When gods forbid, how helpless are men!

    Some ages back,
    The sage Anack
    Courted a frisky Samian body,
    Singing her praise
    In metered phrase
    As flowing as his bowls of toddy.

    Till I was hoarse
    Might I discourse
    Upon the cruelties of Venus;
    'T were waste of time
    As well of rhyme,
    For you've been there yourself, M�cenas!

    Perfect your bliss
    If some fair miss
    Love you yourself and not your min�;
    I, fortune's sport,
    All vainly court
    The beauteous, polyandrous Phryne!
meaning:
Eugene Field's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Tardy Apology - II"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
